title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye swelling
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye swelling are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the eyes. These natural remedies offer a safer and gentler alternative to over-the-counter medications. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with eye swelling include chamomile, goldenseal, and eyebright. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help alleviate eye swelling and irritation. Goldenseal is an antimicrobial herb that can help treat eye infections causing swelling. Eyebright, a powerful astringent, reduces eye swelling and soothes irritation. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from eye discomfort, improving their overall quality of life and ability to perform daily tasks without hindrance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The herb, also known as Euphrasia officinalis, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various eye ailments, including eye swelling. The tincture is made by soaking the eyebright herb in alcohol, which extracts its active compounds. These compounds include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects. When applied topically to the eyes, eyebright tincture can help reduce swelling, soothe irritation, and promote healing. Its astringent properties can also help tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture contains active constituents like tannins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation, making it effective in treating eye swelling.
Astringent: The astringent properties of eyebright, due to its tannin content, help to tighten and shrink swollen tissues around the eyes.
Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture has antimicrobial properties, mainly from its rosmarinic acid content, which can help prevent and treat eye infections that may cause swelling.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including vitamins A, C, and E, found in eyebright tincture, helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the likelihood of eye swelling.
Analgesic: Eyebright tincture contains analgesic compounds, such as salicylic acid, that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds, the eyebright flowers are used in tinctures for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, beneficial for reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: High in therapeutic constituents, the leaves are used in making eye swelling tinctures due to their soothing, anti-inflammatory, and mildly antimicrobial effects.
Seeds: Packed with medicinal potential, the seeds contribute to tinctures for eye swelling relief, thanks to their demulcent and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and stems at dawn or mid-morning when they are most potent.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped euphrasia officinalis with three parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:3 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking it daily to facilitate solute extraction and maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the potent tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Belladonna tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These alkaloids, such as atropine, scopolamine, and hyoscyamine, can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes by relaxing the muscles in the eye area and reducing the production of fluids that can cause swelling. Additionally, belladonna tincture has been traditionally used to soothe eye irritations, including pink eye and styes. However, it's important to note that belladonna tincture should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can have potentially serious side effects if used improperly.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Belladonna tincture can reduce eye swelling by decreasing inflammation, due to the presence of alkaloids like hyoscyamine and scopolamine.
Vasoconstrictor: Belladonna constricts blood vessels, reducing blood flow and swelling in the eyes, thanks to the alkaloids atropine, hyoscyamine, and scopolamine.
Anticholinergic: It inhibits the parasympathetic nervous system, alleviating eye swelling by preventing the release of histamine and other inflammatory substances.
Analgesic: Belladonna tincture can help manage pain associated with eye swelling, as alkaloids like hyoscyamine and scopolamine have pain-relieving properties.
Antispasmodic: Belladonna can relax smooth muscles in the eye area, easing swelling and discomfort, due to the antispasmodic effects of alkaloids like hyoscyamine and scopolamine.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in alkaloids, the flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: Containing atropine and scopolamine, the leaves are used for their anticholinergic properties, helping to alleviate eye swelling.
Roots: Packed with medicinal compounds, the roots are used for their antispasmodic and analgesic properties, providing relief for eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh belladonna leaves and flowers from the wild or cultivated plants.
Rinse the plant material with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and pack them tightly into glass jars.
Add 25% high-proof grain alcohol such as everclear to the jar, covering the plant material completely.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six weeks, shaking the jars daily, before filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the eyes. The herb rue, from which the tincture is derived, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including eye problems. Rue tincture works by constricting the blood vessels in the eyes, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation. Additionally, the tincture has natural antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healing in the eyes. Overall, rue tincture is a natural and effective remedy for reducing eye swelling and promoting eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rue tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of compounds like arborinine and methylnonate, help reduce swelling in the eyes by decreasing inflammation and irritation.

Antihistaminic: The antihistaminic properties of rue tincture, attributed to the alkaloid rutin, help alleviate eye swelling by inhibiting the release and effects of histamines responsible for allergic reactions.
Analgesic: Rue tincture's analgesic effects, resulting from the presence of compounds like pilocarpine, help manage eye swelling by reducing pain and discomfort associated with swelling.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of rue tincture, due to the presence of essential oils like limonene and pinenes, help combat eye swelling by preventing bacterial and viral infections that can cause swelling.

Astringent: Rue tincture's astringent properties, attributed to the tannins present in the plant, help alleviate eye swelling by reducing excess fluid retention and tightening the skin around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and tannins that help alleviate irritation and decrease swelling in the eye area.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids and vitamins, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in eye swelling.
Roots: Packed with medicinal compounds, such as rutin and hesperidin, that help decrease eye swelling and soothe irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rue leaves and stems in early morning or late afternoon for highest essential oil content.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and mix with vodka at a ratio of one part herb to four parts solvent.
Fill a clean glass jar with the herb-vodka mixture, leaving about an inch at the top for expansion during maceration.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily, allowing the tincture to mature and extract.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting herbal tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The herb's active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. Additionally, St. John's wort has antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling. When used as a tincture, the herb's active compounds are easily absorbed into the body, allowing for quick and effective relief of eye swelling. However, it is important to note that St. John's wort can interact with certain medications, so it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before using this herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's Wort tincture reduces inflammation and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the release of inflammatory chemicals, thanks to its active constituents, hypericin and hyperforin.
Analgesic: It alleviates pain associated with eye swelling by desensitizing nerve endings, due to the presence of hypericin and other volatile oils.
Antimicrobial: St. John's Wort tincture helps prevent eye infections that can cause swelling, as it contains hyperforin which disrupts bacterial membranes.
Antioxidant: Its rich antioxidant properties, provided by hypericin, flavonoids, and xanthones, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing swelling and promoting overall eye health.
Astringent: The astringent property of St. John's Wort tincture, derived from tannins, helps tighten and soothe swollen eye tissues, thereby reducing swelling and irritation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, the flowers of St. John's wort are used to make medicinal tinctures for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Containing similar compounds as the flowers, St. John's wort leaves are used in tinctures for eye swelling, as they help reduce inflammation and soothe the affected area.
Buds: High in active constituents, St. John's wort buds are used in medicinal tinctures for eye swelling, as they can help alleviate inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator or air drying method.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine ground hypericum perforatum with vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The herb's active compounds, such as allantoin, aucubin, and mucilage, have been shown to soothe irritation, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms of eye swelling. Plantain tincture can also help reduce redness and discomfort caused by environmental factors, allergies, or infections. Its natural anti-histamine properties can further help manage allergic reactions that lead to eye swelling. Regular use of plantain tincture can provide long-term relief and support overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce eye swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the active constituents such as allantoin, flavonoids, and mucilage.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and constrict tissues, reducing fluid buildup and swelling around the eyes, due to tannins present in the plant.
Antihistamine: Plantain tincture can alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, as it contains compounds that inhibit histamine release, such as kaempferol and quercetin.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture's antimicrobial properties help protect the eye area from infection, which can contribute to swelling, due to active constituents like aucubin and other phenolic acids.
Demulcent: Plantain tincture soothes and protects irritated eye tissues, reducing swelling, by forming a protective film with its mucilaginous constituents, like polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Plantain leaves are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Seeds: Plantain seeds are rich in mucilage, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing irritated eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the plantain plant contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating eye swelling.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant has astringent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves and stems according to your desired dosage.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 100-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:3.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The herb, also known as Calendula officinalis, has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe irritated skin and tissue. When used as a tincture, the active compounds in pot marigold are extracted and concentrated, making it a potent remedy for eye swelling. Its anti-inflammatory effects help to constrict the blood vessels around the eyes, reducing fluid buildup and alleviating swelling. Additionally, pot marigold has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent eye infections, further promoting eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation, which is crucial for treating eye swelling, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help prevent infections that can cause eye swelling, thanks to active constituents such as essential oils and alkaloids.
Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture alleviates pain associated with eye swelling, as it contains pain-relieving compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture protect the eyes from free radical damage, reducing the risk of eye swelling caused by oxidative stress, attributed to active constituents like carotenoids and flavonoids.

Astringent: The astringent property of pot marigold tincture helps reduce eye swelling by tightening and constricting the skin and tissues around the eye, due to the presence of tannins and other astringent compounds.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of pot marigold have calming effects that can alleviate irritation and swelling in the eyes.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce eye swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers on a sunny day for optimal potency and freshness.
Cleanly dry the harvested calendula flowers in a warm oven or dehydrator within hours.
Chop the dried calendula flowers into small pieces and measure 500g using a digital scale.
Combine the chopped calendula with 70% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:2 by weight.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks allowing it to macerate and extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The active compounds in comfrey, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to help decrease inflammation and promote healing. When applied topically to the affected area, comfrey tincture can help alleviate symptoms of eye swelling, such as puffiness and redness. Additionally, comfrey tincture can help soothe dry, itchy eyes and provide relief from discomfort. Its natural healing properties make it a popular alternative to over-the-counter medications for treating eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture helps reduce eye swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory effects.
Analgesic: Comfrey tincture alleviates pain associated with eye swelling due to the presence of tannins, which have astringent and pain-relieving properties.
Astringent: Comfrey tincture constricts and tightens the skin around the eye, reducing swelling, due to its tannin content.

Antioxidant: Comfrey tincture helps protect the skin around the eye from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healing and reducing swelling, as a result of rosmarinic acid and other antioxidant compounds.
Cell proliferation: Comfrey tincture promotes cell growth and tissue regeneration, speeding up the healing process and reducing swelling, due to the presence of allantoin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of allantoin, which helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing, making them ideal for treating eye swelling.
Roots: Rich in rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making the roots useful for creating medicinal tinctures to alleviate eye swelling.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making comfrey flowers a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and stems from healthy plants in the morning.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the clean comfrey material into small pieces to increase surface area for maceration.
Mix 1 part comfrey material with 2 parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks shaking daily to allow infusion and extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, work together to constrict blood vessels and decrease fluid buildup in the eye area. Additionally, yarrow has antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and soothing irritation, yarrow tincture can provide relief for various eye conditions, such as conjunctivitis, styes, and allergies. Its natural and potent benefits make it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituents like flavonoids, helps reduce eye swelling by alleviating inflammation and irritation.
Astringent: The astringent property of yarrow tincture, owed to tannins, is important for eye swelling as it helps constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing fluid buildup and swelling.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic effect, derived from compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, aids in managing eye swelling by alleviating pain and discomfort associated with swollen eyes.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of yarrow tincture, attributed to volatile oils like azulene, is crucial for eye swelling as it helps combat potential infections causing or exacerbating the swelling.

Antioxidant: Yarrow tincture's antioxidant property, due to flavonoids and phenolic acids, is essential for eye swelling as it supports overall eye health by neutralizing free radicals and promoting tissue repair and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory flavonoids, making them ideal for reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: Contain azulene, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for treating eye inflammation.
Seeds: Source of essential fatty acids, which can help alleviate symptoms of eye swelling and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned yarrow plant material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife.
Combine 1 part chopped yarrow with 3 parts 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. Arnica has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various kinds of inflammation and pain. The active compounds in arnica, such as sesquiterpene lactones, help to stimulate the flow of blood and reduce inflammation, making it an effective treatment for eye swelling. Moreover, arnica tincture is a natural remedy that is gentle on the skin and can be used around the delicate eye area without causing irritation. Its soothing properties can help to alleviate discomfort and promote healing, making it an excellent choice for those seeking a natural solution for eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture reduces eye swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and helenalin.
Analgesic: It alleviates pain associated with eye swelling due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and volatile oils.
Vasoconstrictor: Arnica flower tincture helps minimize swelling by constricting blood vessels, containing active constituents like tannins and resins.
Antioxidant: It aids in reducing swelling by neutralizing free radicals, attributable to flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Immune-stimulant: Arnica flower tincture supports the immune system, promoting healing and reducing swelling, through its active constituents like polysaccharides and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain the highest concentration of active compounds, making them ideal for reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: Rich in soothing properties, helping alleviate irritation and reducing swelling.
Buds: Packed with anti-inflammatory agents, buds are effective in treating eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arnica montana flowers at peak bloom when petals are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dry place away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days.
Combine 1 part dried arnica montana flowers with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 6-8 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with eye swelling because it contains properties that can reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels. It has tannins, which have astringent qualities that can help soothe and tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of swelling. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and cooling effects can help alleviate irritation and discomfort, providing relief for puffy or swollen eyes. Its natural and herbal composition makes it a popular choice for those seeking a gentle and effective solution for eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture reduces inflammation and swelling around the eyes by constricting blood vessels and inhibiting the production of inflammatory compounds, thanks to its active constituents, including tannins, gallic acid, and essential oils.
Astringent: Witch hazel has astringent properties that help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and swelling, due to its high tannin content.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tincture contains antioxidants that protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, contributing to reduced swelling and improved skin health, with flavonoids and proanthocyanidins being the main antioxidant compounds.

Soothing: Witch hazel has a soothing effect on irritated skin, helping to alleviate discomfort and itchiness associated with eye swelling, as a result of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, provided by the presence of tannins and volatile oils.

Vasoconstrictive: Witch hazel tincture helps reduce eye swelling by constricting blood vessels, which minimizes the appearance of redness and puffiness, owing to its active constituents, particularly gallic acid and other polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains high levels of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated and swollen eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce eye swelling and soothe irritated eyes.

Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant, also known as "witch hazel rods," contain high levels of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce eye swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel bark and leaves in early spring or fall.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to release essential oils.
Combine one part chopped witch hazel with three parts high-proof vodka in a jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks allowing for maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer to a dark glass bottle.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye swelling with herbal tinctures is by using a chamomile or eyebright tincture. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritation. Mix 2-3 drops of tincture in a cup of water and use it as an eye wash, up to 3 times a day. Additionally, applying a cold compress to the affected eye can further help alleviate swelling. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal tinctures include allergies, conjunctivitis, and styes. For allergies, nettle and eyebright tinctures can help reduce inflammation and irritation. In the case of conjunctivitis, goldenseal and calendula tinctures have antimicrobial properties that can help fight infection. For styes, echinacea tincture can boost the immune system and reduce swelling. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment.
